'''Christopher David Dean''' (born 17 January 1988) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|ce|sr}} forward or on the {{rlp|wg}} in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Dean also played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the top flight, as well as [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the top flight and the second tier.

==Background==
Dean was born in [[Higher End]], [[Metropolitan Borough of Wigan|Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

==Career==
Dean signed for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] aged 15 from the Orrell St James club in the [[Orrell, Greater Manchester|Orrell]] district of [[Metropolitan Borough of Wigan|Wigan]].<ref name=WWprofile/>

Dean made 23 first team appearances for St Helens scoring 10 tries.<ref name=RLP/> In December 2010, [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] announced that Dean had joined the club on a one-year contract from Saints for the 2011 season<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/9275536.stm|title=Wakefield Trinity Wildcats sign St Helens' Chris Dean|publisher=BBC Sport|date=10 December 2010|access-date=23 January 2018}}</ref> in which he picked up 9 tries from 21 appearances.<ref name=RLP/>

In August 2011 it was reported that Dean would join [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] for the 2012 season<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14473847|title=Wakefield Wildcats sign Peter Fox and Ali Lauititi|publisher=BBC Sport|date=10 August 2011|accessdate=23 January 2018}}</ref> having previously had two loan spells at the club. Dean signed a three-year extension with Widnes in October 2014,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29617562|title=Chris Dean: Widnes Vikings centre signs new contract|publisher=BBC Sport|date=14 October 2014|accessdate=23 January 2018}}</ref> followed in 2016 by a further extension until the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/10229982/chris-dean-signs-new-deal-with-widnes-vikings|title=Chris Dean signs new deal with Widnes Vikings|publisher=Sky Sports|date=4 April 2016}}</ref>
